# Copilot Instructions

Trust these instructions and only search the codebase if the information here is incomplete or found to be incorrect.

## Project Summary

**Convert to Wealthfolio** is a Node.js CLI tool (TypeScript, CommonJS) that converts CSV files from various brokerage formats into the import format for [Wealthfolio](https://wealthfolio.app) portfolio tracker (v3.x). It uses a plugin-based architecture for format support and pluggable data providers for symbol resolution.

- **Language**: TypeScript 5.x, compiled to ES2020 CommonJS (`dist/`)
- **Runtime**: Node.js 22+ (CI tests 22.x, 24.x, 25.x)
- **Package manager**: npm (use `npm ci` for clean installs)
- **Key runtime deps**: `commander`, `csv-parse`, `csv-stringify`, `colorette`, `ini`
- **Key dev deps**: `jest`/`ts-jest`, `eslint` (v10 flat config), `prettier`

## Build & Validate — Exact Command Sequence

Always run `npm ci` before building if `node_modules` may be out of date or missing.

```bash
npm ci                  # install deps (use ci, not install, to match CI)
npm run build           # compiles src/ → dist/ via tsc (no output = success)
npm test                # runs Jest (152 tests, ~3s, all must pass)
npm run lint:check      # ESLint flat config — no output = success
npm run format:check    # Prettier — "All matched files use Prettier code style!" = success
```

**CI pipeline** (`.github/workflows/build-and-test.yml`) runs on every push/PR in this order:

1. `npm ci`
2. `npm run lint:check && npm run format:check`
3. `npm run build`
4. `npm test`

All four steps must pass before a PR can be merged. Replicate this locally before pushing.

**Fix formatting automatically**: `npm run format:fix` (runs Prettier with `--write`). **Fix lint automatically**: `npm run lint:fix` (runs ESLint with `--fix`).

## Code Style & Lint Requirements

Every `.ts` and `.js` file **must** start with this block comment (ESLint `header` rule, enforced as an error). Extra comma-separated authors may be appended after `Oleksii Serdiuk` (e.g. `Copyright (c) 2026 Oleksii Serdiuk, Your Name`):

```ts
/*!
 * Copyright (c) 2026 Oleksii Serdiuk[, Additional Author[, ...]]
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
 */
```

Every `.md` file **must** start with this HTML comment block (also enforced by ESLint). The same optional extra-authors rule applies:

```html
<!--
Copyright (c) 2026 Oleksii Serdiuk[, Additional Author[, ...]]
SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
-->
```

Additional enforced rules (errors, not warnings):

- 2-space indentation, double quotes, semicolons, Unix line endings
- Trailing commas on multiline arrays/objects/functions/imports
- `prefer-const`, `eqeqeq`, `curly` (always), `no-console`
- `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` is an **error** in production code (warning in tests)
- Unused vars allowed only with `_` prefix

## Project Layout

```text
src/
  index.ts              # CLI entry point (commander setup, commands)
  core/
    BaseFormat.ts       # Abstract base class for format plugins + WealthfolioRecord interface
    Converter.ts        # Main orchestrator: parse → detect/validate → convert → write CSV
    DataProvider.ts     # Abstract base class for symbol data provider plugins
    FieldRequirements.ts # Per-activity-type field validation rules
    Logger.ts           # Singleton logger with colorized output and log levels
    SymbolDataService.ts # Manages and queries registered DataProvider instances
    Utils.ts            # Utility functions (e.g., roundToPrecision)
  formats/
    index.ts            # Format plugin registry (array exported as default)
    GenericFormat.ts    # Generic CSV format plugin (kept last in registry — most permissive)
  data-providers/
    index.ts            # Data provider exports
    OverridesDataProvider.ts # INI file-based symbol override/lookup provider
tests/                  # Jest tests mirroring src/ structure — all in tests/, not src/
  core/
  formats/
  data-providers/
dist/                   # Compiled output (git-ignored, generated by npm run build)
docs/                   # User and developer guides
  plugin-development-guide.md
  data-provider-development-guide.md
  generic-format-user-guide.md
examples/               # Sample CSV and INI files for manual testing
.github/
  workflows/build-and-test.yml  # CI pipeline
eslint.config.mjs       # ESLint flat config (covers .ts, .js, .md, .yml)
jest.config.js          # Jest config (ts-jest preset, tests/ root, tsconfig.test.json)
tsconfig.json           # Production TS config (src/ → dist/, ES2020, strict)
tsconfig.test.json      # Test TS config (extends tsconfig.json, includes tests/, noEmit)
```

## Architecture: Adding Format Plugins

1. Create `src/formats/MyFormat.ts` extending `BaseFormat` (implement `validate`, `convert`, `getExpectedSchema`).
2. Register in `src/formats/index.ts` by adding `new MyFormat()` to the array.
   - **Important**: `GenericFormat` must remain last — it is the most permissive and will match almost anything.
3. Add tests in `tests/formats/MyFormat.test.ts`. Aim for 100% coverage on new code.
4. See `docs/plugin-development-guide.md` for the full guide.

## Architecture: Adding Data Provider Plugins

1. Create `src/data-providers/MyProvider.ts` extending `DataProvider` (implement `canHandle`, `querySymbol`).
2. Export from `src/data-providers/index.ts`.
3. See `docs/data-provider-development-guide.md` for the full guide.

## Testing

- Tests live in `tests/` (not co-located with source).
- Config: `jest.config.js` using `ts-jest` with `tsconfig.test.json`.
- Run: `npm test` (all suites) or `npm run test:coverage` for a coverage report.
- Coverage is collected from `src/**/*.ts` excluding `index.ts` files and `.d.ts`.
- `no-console` is enforced — use `Logger.getInstance()` for output in production code.
